channel.title: The Daily Star
channel.description: Latest news from The Daily Star

‘Where are you?’ The afterlife of a song that outlived borders

The iconic duo that anchors Eid celebrations across Bangladesh
Dhaka in holiday mode: Post-Eid crowds pack zoo, parks, metro rail
Afran Nisho-starrer ‘Domm’ set for international premiere
Amorim had great ideas but they did not click at Man United, says Maguire
Independence Day 2026 / The unfinished truth of 1971: Genocide, mass rape, and justice
Musings / A fall that is not autumn
The day we became independent
Independence and its discontents: For whom was 1971?
item.description: The emergence of a Muslim-majority Bangladesh grounded in ethno-linguistic Bangalee nationalism delivers a clear historical verdict: language, culture, and lived social realities matter more than theological or statist abstractions.
item.dc:creator: Azfar Hussain

item.title: Rein in errant clinics and diagnostic centres
When 1971 enters the feed
item.description: The politics of memory is never only about the past. It concerns who gets to define the nation through selective remembrance and selective silence, and which parts of the history are elevated, ritualised, or pushed aside.
item.dc:creator: Khairul Hassan Jahin

item.title: Govt seeks extradition of Hadi murder accused from India: Home minister
Bangladeshis in Saudi Arabia with expired visas asked to leave by April 18
Update / Bus plunges into Padma: 26 bodies recovered so far
JD Vance likely to visit Pakistan for talks over war on Iran: report
Nepal report into deadly uprising recommends prosecuting ex-PM
item.description: At least 77 people were killed in the anti-corruption youth uprising on September 8 and 9, which began over a brief social media ban, but tapped into longstanding fury over economic hardship

item.title: Caught in the crossfire: Is Iraq able to balance Iran and US?
item.description: Can the balance survive as the conflict escalates?

item.title: Fuel queues eating into earning hours
Aspirations of independence remain unmet: Nahid Islam
Former DGFI chief Sheikh Mamun Khaled detained by DB
item.description: Arrest made in Mirpur DOHS; accused in International Crimes Tribunal case

item.title: Moon race: How China is challenging the US
Italy's World Cup play-off 'biggest match' of career: Gattuso
To save a flower: Bangladesh’s birth from lyrics and bullets
IMF ready to assist Bangladesh in the pursuit of sustained stabilisation and growth: Asia Pacific director
Viral / How seven missing dogs walked 17 km home and became internet stars
Independence Day 2026 / The unfinished truth of 1971: Genocide, mass rape, and justice
item.description: By MA Hasan
item.dc:creator: MA Hasan

item.title: Independence and its discontents: For whom was 1971?
item.description: History, too, bristles up, staging and restaging questions and concerns that remain unresolved. They persist, stubbornly, as we continue to inhabit a conjuncture marked by chasms and contradictions: between rhetoric and reality, between what has been and what is yet to be.
item.dc:creator: Azfar Hussain

item.title: Interactive / Operation Searchlight: The night hell opened over Dhaka
Iran says 'no negotiations' as US warns to accept 15-point deal

Fire breaks out on moving Chattala Express in Ctg; two coaches damaged
Shafiqur pays tribute to Liberation War martyrs, prays for country’s sovereignty
Live / Independence Day parade underway at National Parade Square
item.description: President Mohammed Shahabuddin and Prime Minister Tarique Rahman are attending the event.

item.title: Iran says it is reviewing a US ceasefire plan but no talks; Trump says Tehran leaders want a deal
Six in PSL signals shifting tide
item.description: For years, all-rounder Shakib Al Hasan and pacer Mustafizur Rahman were Bangladesh’s only regulars in franchise-based T20 leagues around the world before leg-spinner Rishad Hossain joined their ranks with recent appearances in the Pakistan Super League (PSL) and Big Bash League (BBL).

item.title: Vinicius Jr eager to replicate Real Madrid success with Brazil
item.description: "I feel more at ease, I'm happier, and when I'm happier, everyone around me is happier and more confident too," Vinicius told reporters ahead of Thursday's friendly match against France

item.title: Witnesses to genocide
‘Audiences are the ones who keep cinema alive’: Siam Ahmed
Senegal lodge appeal to Court of Arbitration for Sport over AFCON final decision
The naval hero who envisioned a nation
item.description: Moazzem became a leading proponent of Bengali rights, eventually becoming the focal point of the infamous ‘Agartala Conspiracy Case’.
item.dc:creator: Ystiaque Ahmed

item.title: National Genocide Remembrance Day / Operation Searchlight and the unrecognised genocide of 1971
item.description: By Tanziral Dilshad Ditan
item.dc:creator: Tanziral Dilshad Ditan

item.title: Echoes of Anne Frank in Swati’s war diary
item.description: Morning does not bring relief. It brings curfew. The city becomes a prison of smoke and silence. From behind closed doors, they glimpse fires still raging, hear distant shots. Anyone who dared step outside, she records, was shot.
item.dc:creator: Miftahul Jannat

item.title: No hike in fuel prices for now
Iran rejects US ceasefire plan
Bus plunges into Padma: 18 bodies recovered so far
Independence Day today
'My daughter drowned while she was on the phone with me!'
item.description: At Daulatdia ferry ghat, a grieving mother recalls her final phone call with daughter, who remains missing after a bus plunged into the Padma

item.title: Some Gulf states and Jordan urge Iraq to stop attacks launched from its territory
Gold prices drop by Tk 36,000 per bhori in three weeks
item.description: Domestic gold prices have fallen by nearly Tk 36,000 per bhori over the past 23 days, driven by a sharp decline in global rates amid shifting geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

One-third of listed firms saw sales decline in FY25

item.description: Proposed reforms include completing both public exams by Dec 31, introducing uniform HSC question papers, and expanding CCTV monitoring in exam rooms

item.description: One‑minute campus blackout held at 10:30pm

item.description: Recognised for using genomics to help guide a mass typhoid vaccine rollout that has already reached more than 40 million children

item.title: Iran military says fired missiles at US aircraft carrier
item.description: The Iranian Navy's Qader cruise missiles targeted the USS Abraham Lincoln, the statement said

item.description: "Means everything and pinch-me moment to play for your country. Special feeling and humbling," said player of the series Esterhuizen.

item.title: Iranian strikes pose ‘existential threat’, Gulf states tell UN

Cabrera to 'assess and consider Hamza's availability' for Vietnam match
Western powers were unable to secure shipping in the Red Sea. Hormuz will be harder
How much more credibility can the US afford to lose?
item.description: For nearly two decades, global political discourse was held captive by a single assertion: Iran stood on the brink of becoming a nuclear weapons state. The warnings were constant, urgent, and perpetually imminent, yet each passing year renewed the alarm without seeing the predicted outcome.
item.dc:creator: Abdullah A Dewan

item.title: 15% journalists report workplace sexual harassment, women face higher rates: survey

item.description: Final schedule for 2025 primary scholarship exam announced, hill districts get separate timetable

Most listed firms pay less than 5% dividends for FY25

item.description: After the uprising that toppled the Awami League government, the interim government removed all President’s press wing officials

item.description: Body sent for autopsy as police attempt to establish identity

Eid without loved ones’ embrace
The Bangladesh delta plan: Between certainty and reality
item.description: The models were not wrong, exactly. The science was sound. But the models were answering questions that nobody in that room had asked. We had travelled eleven hours to show people a mirror, and the mirror was reflecting something they did not recognise as themselves. It was reflecting us, the experts, not them, the locals.
item.dc:creator: Willem van Deursen

item.title: Another Eid, another train tragedy

item.description: The live broadcast from Seoul's Gwanghwamun Square reached Netflix's weekly Top 10 in 80 countries and secured the number one spot in 24 countries

item.description: The current discussions surrounding the July National Charter (Constitutional Reform) Implementation Order, 2025 stems largely from its nomenclature.

item.description: The 31-year-old, who scored 202 runs at an average of 20.20 during the Ashes, was bought by Delhi Capitals for 20 million rupees ($213,167) at the auction.

item.title: New Zealand players willing to play World Cup opener against Iran outside US
item.description: Iran were one of the first nations to qualify for the finals but their participation has been in doubt since the U.S.-Israeli conflict with the Islamic Republic broke out at the end of February.

item.description: 14 March of this year marked the 143rd death anniversary of Karl Marx, a revolutionary philosopher whom I do not need to introduce to my readers.

item.description: Runner Group’s subsidiary, Runner Automobiles PLC, is set to assemble electric vehicles (EVs) of Chinese automaker BYD in Bangladesh, marking a significant step forward for the country’s automobile industry.

Bangladesh faces $4.8b surge in annual energy import bill
item.description: Bangladesh’s annual fossil fuel import bill is projected to soar by $4.8 billion, a 40 percent increase from 2025 levels, due to the Middle East crisis, according to a new analysis by Zero Carbon Analytics (ZCA).

item.title: From ash to toothpaste: Bangladesh’s oral care shift
item.description: In her childhood, Anwara Akhter would begin the day by collecting mango leaves or cutting a neem twig. She would chew one end until it frayed into bristles, then use the fibrous tip to scrub her teeth.

item.description: Belgium's all-time leading goalscorer Romelu Lukaku will miss his country's visit to the United States for two 2026 World Cup warm-up matches, the Belgian football federation (RBFA) announced on Tuesday.

item.description: Against Madani’s Indian nationalism, Jinnah’s argument was that the historical interaction and proximity between Hindus and Muslims in Indian society existed only at the level of external social life.
